summarization.a: The chart shows the changes in In:Ga:As atomic percentage and Cl atomic percentage on the surface over time during Cl adsorption.

data_extraction.a:
| Cl Adsorption Time (sec) | In Atomic Percentage (%) | Ga Atomic Percentage (%) |As Atomic Percentage (%) |Cl Atomic Percentage (%) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Pristine | 45 |36|15| 0 |
| 2.5 | 48 | 39|14|7 |
| 5 | 49 |35|15|12 |
| 10 | 49 |35|15|16 |
| 20 | 46 | 35|16 |16|

a.question: Does the composition of bulk InGaAs change upon chlorine exposure?
a.answer: a	Eventhough there is a slight increase in indium content for short times, the overal ratio remains constant, so it does not affect the ratios.

a.question: Why does the chlorine content saturate?
a.answer: a	The chlorine is only added to the surface. If the full surface is covered in chlorine, there cannot be any additional chlorine added.

a.question: What is the saturation time for chlorine exposure?
